title: euphorbia cassia subsp. rigoi
intro: Euphorbia cassia subsp. rigoi, also known as Rigoi's spurge, is a succulent plant species within the Euphorbia genus. It is a shrub-like plant native to the island of Madagascar, characterized by its small white flowers and reddish-brown stems. Euphorbia cassia subsp. rigoi has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly for its anti-inflammatory properties, which have been attributed to the presence of diterpenoids and other bioactive compounds. The plant has been used to prepare herbal remedies for various health conditions, including fever reduction, skin irritations, and pain relief, and is often consumed as a tea or applied topically in the form of a salve or ointment.
